description: Services are not directed to children under 13; ensure rapid removal if discovered. COPPA.
system_behavior: Age gates; promptly delete on discovery; custodial/UTMA accounts treated as adult-owner NPPI.
edge_cases: Teen accounts only if product/legal explicitly allow.
alerts_metrics: Under-age attempts blocked.
why_reg_cite: Services are not directed to children under 13; ensure rapid removal if discovered. COPPA.

name: Policy Scope
description: Define what products/channels/partners and systems are in/out of scope for resolution actions
system_behavior: Maintain a machine-readable Scope Registry enumerating products, channels, vendors, and systems covered by resolution actions; expose scope to control engines so freezes/limits/exports apply deterministically.
edge_cases: Shadow IT or partner-owned sub-processors—require explicit inclusion or documented exception; joint/fintech programs must specify who executes freezes and exports.
alerts_metrics: Scope coverage % (controls mapped to all in-scope systems); age of last scope review; count of open scope exceptions.
why_reg_cite: Safety-and-soundness requirements expect clear applicability and program boundaries so controls can be executed and tested (12 CFR Part 741; 12 CFR §748.0–§748.2).

name: Early-Warning Indicators
description: Detect trouble early and stage response
system_behavior: Maintain a ruleset that flips the org into Prepared or Elevated posture when thresholds breach.

slas.description: ** Evaluate indicators hourly during business hours; daily after hours.

edge_cases: False positives damped via two-consecutive-interval rule.
alerts_metrics: Pager alert for posture “Elevated”; dashboard of breaches per week.
why_reg_cite: Enable timely action ahead of conservatorship/liquidation; safety/soundness expectations (12 CFR Part 741; FCUA §1786/§1787).

name: Safe-Mode Transaction Controls
description: Rapidly limit risk while maintaining access
system_behavior: Toggle Safe-Mode feature flag: set transaction limits, enable allowlists, disable new high-risk actions, preserve read-only access.

slas.description: ** Apply within ≤ 60 minutes of trigger; propagate to all processors ≤ 30 minutes after core change.

edge_cases: Ensure critical inbound credits (e.g., payroll, benefits) remain open (allowlist).
alerts_metrics: Monitor decline rates, member contact volume, cash levels.
why_reg_cite: Reduce run/operational risk while maintaining member access; integrates with security program (12 CFR Part 748).

name: Targeted Account Freeze
system_behavior: Freeze specified member accounts/entities; block debits; allow regulator-approved credits; display freeze reason codes.

slas.description: ** ≤ 30 minutes from approval to effective block.

edge_cases: Joint accounts; fiduciary accounts; legal garnishments take precedence.
alerts_metrics: Count of frozen accounts; median time-to-freeze.
why_reg_cite: Preserve assets and comply with directives; resolution context under FCUA §1787; integrated with §748 program.

name: Institution-Wide Freeze
system_behavior: Set global state FROZEN; block new liabilities; allow limited inbound credits per regulator guidance; lock new account onboarding.
edge_cases: Offline channels (ATM) sync; third-party batch cutoffs.
alerts_metrics: Time to full propagation; number of failed auths by channel.
why_reg_cite: Execute immediate, organization-level freeze when directed by Board emergency action or NCUA (FCUA §1786/§1787).

name: Next-Business-Day Availability
system_behavior: By next business day, ensure member portal/API supports: balance inquiry, statement download, claim instructions, and contact routes; enable read-only mode if needed.

slas.description: ** Portal available by next local business open.

edge_cases: DNS/hosting cutovers; CDN cache; ADA accessibility baseline.
alerts_metrics: Uptime; page latency; call abandonment rate.
why_reg_cite: Maintain essential member access and information during stress; insured member expectations under §1787; safety/soundness (Part 741).

name: Trustee/Conservator Handover
description: Standardize a clean transfer to NCUA trustee
system_behavior: Generate and deliver a standardized Handover Packet and grant scoped access to systems.

slas.description: ** Packet initial version ≤ 4 hours; full delivery ≤ 24 hours.

edge_cases: Conflicting legal holds; vendor refusals—escalate to Legal + NCUA.
alerts_metrics: Time to first successful trustee login; missing artifact count.
why_reg_cite: Enable orderly transfer of operations to NCUA-appointed conservator/liquidating agent (FCUA §1786(h), §1787; 12 CFR Part 709).

name: Records Preservation for Resolution
system_behavior: Build a Resolution Records Package with member/share/loan ledgers, ACH/card histories, GL, governance minutes, contracts, and BSA logs.

slas.description: ** Start ≤ 2 hours; complete ≤ 24 hours; monthly snapshots retained.

edge_cases: Incomplete vendor exports—document and request extension via NCUA.
alerts_metrics: Export completeness %; restore-test success/failure.
why_reg_cite: Preserve and produce reproducible records per Part 749; support claims and payout under Part 709/§1787.

name: Testing & Validation
system_behavior: Run a structured test program covering safe-mode, freeze propagation, failover, and records restoration.
edge_cases: Production freeze during business hours—simulate off-peak.
alerts_metrics: RTO/RPO met %; propagation time p95; restore success rate.
why_reg_cite: Part 748 requires a security/incident program; safety/soundness demands demonstrable continuity and response.

name: Governance & Review Cadence
system_behavior: Maintain a RACI for resolution actions; ensure quarterly reviews and immediate updates after material events.
edge_cases: Conflicts with vendor SLAs—log exception and mitigation.
alerts_metrics: Open action items; aging exceptions; training compliance %.
why_reg_cite: Clarify decision rights and oversight (FCUA; 12 CFR Part 741; §748.2).

name: Prohibition & Protected Bases
system_behavior: System and staff must not use protected traits or proxies at any stage; avoid unjustified disparate impact and redlining.\
edge_cases: Neighborhood/ZIP as proxy; property-age/location as proxy (real-estate).\
alerts_metrics: Exception rate; proxy variable flags.
why_reg_cite: Reg B §1002.4; FHA; NCUA 701.31\

name: Permissible Inquiries
system_behavior: Limit questions on spouse, marital status, sex, childbearing, immigration to what’s permitted; present required disclosures (e.g., alimony/child support need not be disclosed unless relied upon).\

slas.description: Disclosures shown **before** sensitive fields are collected.\

edge_cases: Joint vs individual apps; visual observation when GMI refused.\
alerts_metrics: % apps with proper disclosures (target 100%).

name: Evaluation & Pricing Rules
system_behavior: Use demonstrably/statistically sound scoring or documented judgmental criteria; never assign negative factors for elderly; treat public-assistance income equally; require pricing exception capture/approval.\
edge_cases: Term exceeds life expectancy; collateral adequacy; telephone listing not considered.\
alerts_metrics: Exception rate %; elderly negatives (target 0).\

name: Appraisal Independence & ROV
system_behavior: Do not rely on biased appraisals; provide reconsideration-of-value (ROV) pathway and log outcomes.\

slas.description: ROV review within **15 days** of request.\

edge_cases: Multiple valuations; AVMs.\
alerts_metrics: # ROVs; % value changes.

name: Action-Taken Notices
system_behavior: Generate and send notices per timing matrix; include score block when a score is used; handle joint/guarantor and business-credit nuances.\
edge_cases: Separate score disclosures for co-applicants; oral permissible for certain small-business phone apps (log rights disclosure).\
alerts_metrics: On-time rate ≥ 99.5%; breach count 0.
why_reg_cite: Reg B §1002.9; FCRA\

name: Government Monitoring (GMI/HMDA)
system_behavior: For covered transactions, ask but do not require GMI; if declined, record via visual/surname rule where required; maintain LAR accuracy.\
edge_cases: Mail/phone apps; multiple applicants.\
alerts_metrics: LAR error rate below threshold.
why_reg_cite: Reg B §1002.13; Reg C\

name: Advertising & Fair Housing
system_behavior: Enforce trigger-term disclosures; apply Fair Housing legend for real-estate ads; prohibit exclusionary geo-targeting; require pre-flight checklist.\
edge_cases: Social/dynamic ads; co-marketing partners.\
alerts_metrics: % ads with checklist (target 100%).

name: LO Compensation & Anti-Steering
system_behavior: Prohibit compensation based on loan terms/proxies; present/document meaningful alternatives (e.g., lowest rate, lowest fees, lowest total cost); block finalization without evidence.\
edge_cases: <3 eligible options → Compliance waiver required.\
alerts_metrics: Evidence rate 100%; exception trend.

name: Third-Party Fair-Lending Oversight
description: ECOA liability flows to creditor; NCUA TPRM\
system_behavior: Perform due diligence at onboarding; require monthly Fair-Lending MI pack (apps/approvals/pricing/exceptions/complaints) from vendors; escalate corrective actions.\

slas.description: MI due by **5th business day** monthly.\

edge_cases: Brokers/lead gens/appraisers/CRAs.\
alerts_metrics: MI on-time rate; unresolved CAPs.
why_reg_cite: ECOA liability flows to creditor; NCUA TPRM\

name: Monitoring & Reviews
system_behavior: Quarterly disparity analytics (apps/approvals/price/terms/denials/exceptions); annual redlining review; Board reporting with corrective actions.\
edge_cases: Low-volume segments → qualitative review.\
alerts_metrics: Disparity deltas beyond thresholds trigger CAP.
why_reg_cite: Program effectiveness & Board oversight\

name: Training
system_behavior: Role-based onboarding + annual; track completion; refresh on rule/product changes.\
edge_cases: Contractors/third parties included.\
alerts_metrics: Completion ≥ 98%.
why_reg_cite: Control effectiveness\

name: Record Retention
system_behavior: Retain specified records; extend on litigation/investigation hold.\

slas.description:
— Retention Grid:**

* Applications/decisions (consumer): **25 months**
* Applications/decisions (business ≤ $1MM): **12 months** _(oral allowed in some cases; keep logs)_
* Certain business credit (>

edge_cases: Self-tests retained 25 months from completion.   AUDIT LOGS: `retention.clock_started`, `legal.hold_issued`, `legal.hold_released`   ACCESS CONTROL: Compliance + Legal.
why_reg_cite: Reg B §1002.12; Reg C\

name: Governance & Delegation
system_behavior: Maintain a living policy; Board approves limits schedule; cash-risk KRIs feed the AML governance dashboard; exceptions tracked and reported via BSA governance cadence.
edge_cases: Interim regulatory change—Chair ratifies; Board at next meeting.
alerts_metrics: Policy age; open exceptions past due; AML control mapping completeness.
why_reg_cite: Board governance under FCUA; written security and BSA programs under 12 CFR §748.1–§748.2; AML program elements (internal controls, independent testing, training) under 31 U.S.C. §5318(h) and 31 CFR §1020.210.

name: Scope & Applicability
system_behavior: Tag covered employees (tellers, vault custodians, branch managers, ops/accounting, armored-courier liaisons, ATM/ITM custodians), activities (receipt, disbursement, shipment, storage, reconciliation, discrepancy handling, training, emergency/disaster), and locations/channels (branches, ops center, ATMs/ITMs, recyclers, night depository).

slas.description: ** Update before go-live of any new asset/role.

edge_cases: Small sites—document compensating reviews and BSA monitoring ties.
alerts_metrics: % assets with assigned custodians/limits; % mapped to AML controls.
why_reg_cite: FCUA governance and §748.1 require comprehensive coverage; AML program must cover all relevant lines (31 CFR §1020.210).

name: Enterprise Cash Limit
description: Cap total cash vs. assets; invest excess
system_behavior: Enforce Total Cash ≤ {X%\} of total assets; auto-notify Treasury to invest excess; post breach flags to AML monitoring context.
edge_cases: Seasonal needs—use [CA-10](cash-control.md#ca-10-seasonal-deviations--exceptions).
alerts_metrics: Days over cap; max deviation; bond headroom.
why_reg_cite: Prudently limit cash; ensure bond coverage (Part 713); inform AML liquidity/anomaly context (31 CFR §1020.210 internal controls).

name: Location & Device Cash Limits
system_behavior: Maintain per-asset limits: Vault ${vault\_limit}, Teller ${teller\_limit}, ATM ${atm\_limit}, ITM/VTM ${itm\_limit}, Recycler ${recycler\_limit}, Petty ${petty\_limit}; system warns/blocks loads above limit without exception ticket; feed exception telemetry to AML case mgmt.
edge_cases: Service outage—manual log with two signatures; back-entry within 1 business day.
alerts_metrics: Breach attempts; exception frequency by site; AML exception count.
why_reg_cite: Prevent excessive exposure per vault/teller/ATM/ITM/recycler/petty fund; align to bond and security expectations (§748.1, Part 713); tie exceptions to AML internal control reviews (31 CFR §1020.210).

name: Dual Control, Keys & Combos
system_behavior: Enforce dual control for vault access, shipments, ATM/ITM loads, night-drop retrieval; maintain key/combination custodian registry; rotate on personnel change or every 90 days; sealed backups.
edge_cases: Emergency access—incident ticket; post-event review.
alerts_metrics: Overdue rotations; single-custodian attempts blocked.
why_reg_cite: Physical/operational controls (§748.1); AML internal controls mandate (31 CFR §1020.210).

name: Reconciliation & GL Controls
system_behavior: Daily reconcile teller, vault, ATM/ITM, recyclers, petty to GL and subsidiary ledgers; zero-balance suspense within {X} days; require documentation for all postings; provide AML investigators access to reconciliation evidence.

slas.description: ** Same-day tie-out; suspense aging reviewed daily.

edge_cases: System outage → manual count; back-post with dual approval.
alerts_metrics: Late recons; suspense aging > {X} days.
why_reg_cite: Reliable records and retrievability (Part 749); AML internal controls & independent testing rely on reconciliations (31 CFR §1020.210).

name: Over/Short Monitoring
system_behavior: Track over/short per person and location; set thresholds for coaching/discipline; analyze patterns; escalate large or recurring items; auto-signal repeated anomalies to AML case mgmt.

slas.description: ** Investigate within **1 business day**; report monthly.

edge_cases: Repeated near-limit shorts—initiate retraining/rotation.
alerts_metrics: Top N outliers; repeat-offender count; AML referrals.
why_reg_cite: Detect losses/embezzlement; governance visibility (§748.1, Part 715); anomalies feed AML monitoring (31 CFR §1020.210).

name: ATM/ITM/Night-Drop & Shipments
system_behavior: Dual-control load/retrieval; cassette totals recorded; night-drop logged with seal capture and (where available) video; cash shipments (inbound/outbound) logged, sealed, countersigned with courier receipts; match courier/fed credit to GL same day.

slas.description: ** Post night-drop **same day**; ATM/ITM balance at service completion; shipments verified and logged same day.

edge_cases: Seal mismatch—stop, escalate, incident ticket; shipment discrepancy—immediate count and courier escalation.
alerts_metrics: Seal exceptions; shipment discrepancy rate; time-to-post.
why_reg_cite: Secure handling and chain of custody (§748.1); audits (Part 715); AML internal controls require documented, testable procedures (31 CFR §1020.210).

name: Surprise Cash Counts & Audits
system_behavior: Perform surprise counts across tellers, vaults, devices at least monthly per site; Supervisory Committee/independent audit conducts periodic reviews; feed results to AML independent testing workpapers; remediate findings.

slas.description: ** Variances resolved within **1 business day**; findings closed by due date.

edge_cases: Inability to count—document; reschedule within 48h.
alerts_metrics: Count coverage %; open findings aging.
why_reg_cite: Independent verification (Part 715); AML independent testing leverages count results (31 CFR §1020.210).

name: Seasonal Deviations & Exceptions
system_behavior: Formal deviation memo to Board: reason, duration, revised limits, bond/insurance adjustments; system whitelists temporary limits with expiry; AML exception log updated.

slas.description: ** Approval **before** exceeding limits; sunset on end date.

edge_cases: Emergency surge—Chair + CEO interim approval, ratify next Board.
alerts_metrics: Active deviations; days since last review.
why_reg_cite: Temporary higher cash needs must be documented and bonded (Part 713); AML governance demands documented exceptions (31 CFR §1020.210).

name: Training & Competency
system_behavior: Initial + annual training: policy/limits, handling accuracy, counterfeit detection, fraud schemes, emergency/robbery response, dual control, device & shipment procedures; role-specific proficiency checks; training completion feeds AML program metrics.
edge_cases: Failed proficiency—remediation and retest.
alerts_metrics: Completion rate; proficiency scores; overdue training counts.
why_reg_cite: BSA program requires training (31 U.S.C. §5318(h); 31 CFR §1020.210); security program effectiveness depends on trained staff (§748.1).

name: Monitoring, Reporting & Recordkeeping
system_behavior: Monthly KPIs/KRIs (limit breaches, late recons, over/short trends, shipment discrepancies, device exceptions); exception log with officer attestation; retain evidence (count sheets, load/ship logs, dual-control logs, recon packs, courier tickets, audit/exam reports, Board materials) per schedule; provide exports for AML independent testing and examiner requests.

slas.description: ** Publish within **15 days** of month end; retention **≥ {X years}** per record type.

edge_cases: Litigation hold supersedes purge.
alerts_metrics: On-time report %; retrieval success rate; open exceptions aging.
why_reg_cite: Ongoing oversight; evidence retention (12 CFR §748.2, Part 749); AML program requires internal controls & independent testing (31 CFR §1020.210).

name: Policy Scope & Risk Appetite
system_behavior: Enforce a single liquidity standard across \{{SCOPE\}}; align ALCO dashboards and alerts to limits.
edge_cases: BaaS partner launches with novel flow → temporary overlay documented.
alerts_metrics: (metric.policy\_past\_due), (metric.limit\_waivers\_open).
why_reg_cite: Board-approved liquidity program and CFP are required (12 CFR §741.12).

name: Definitions & Ratios Catalogue
system_behavior: Central library for LAR, cumulative mismatch, survival horizon, concentration.

slas.description: ** Keep formulas synced with GL mapping **daily**.

edge_cases: Data source outage → fallback to prior day with flag.
alerts_metrics: (metric.schema\_drift\_detected).
why_reg_cite: Common definitions ensure consistent measurement/reporting (§741.12).

name: Maturity Mismatch Limits
system_behavior: Compute cumulative gaps in buckets: O/N, 2–7d, 8–30d, 31–90d, 91–365d, >1y; compare to limits.
edge_cases: Large unscheduled payoff → recalc intra-day.
alerts_metrics: (alert.gap\_breach), (metric.days\_negative\_gap).
why_reg_cite: Governance over cash-flow gaps is core to liquidity safety (§741.12).

name: Survival Horizon Targets
system_behavior: Model survival days for idiosyncratic and combined stress.
edge_cases: Facility operational issue → remove and re-run.
alerts_metrics: (alert.survival\_below\_target).
why_reg_cite: Ability to meet obligations under stress is required (§741.12).

name: Liquid Assets Ratio Bands
system_behavior: Compute LAR and classify: Normal ≥10%; Watch <10%; Low <8%; Critical <6% (policy-set).

slas.description: ** Daily by 16:00; alerts real-time on breach.

edge_cases: Late custodian file → provisional LAR with flag.
alerts_metrics: (alert.lar\_watch), (alert.lar\_low), (alert.lar\_critical).
why_reg_cite: Internal thresholds provide early warning and map to CFP (§741.12).

name: Funding Concentration Limits
system_behavior: Track top 10 depositors; single-provider and facility reliance limits.
edge_cases: Temporarily exceed limit during crisis → document CFP linkage.
alerts_metrics: (alert.concentration\_exceed).
why_reg_cite: Prevent over-reliance on single sources (§741.12).

name: Stress Testing
system_behavior: Run idiosyncratic, systemic, combined; include intraday peaks and BaaS shocks.
edge_cases: Data stale → mark and proceed with conservative overlays.
alerts_metrics: (metric.stress\_on\_time).
why_reg_cite: CFP must be supported by credible stress testing (§741.12).

name: Data & Model Governance
system_behavior: Maintain lineage; reconcile to GL; catalog assumptions.
edge_cases: Partial day outages → flag and annotate KPIs.
alerts_metrics: (metric.recon\_breaks).
why_reg_cite: Reliable inputs and validated methods (§741.12).

name: Reporting Cadence
description: Daily ops, weekly ALCO, quarterly Board.
system_behavior: Auto-generate daily ops pack; weekly ALCO digest; quarterly Board deck.
edge_cases: Holiday shifts → move to prior business day.
alerts_metrics: (metric.report\_ontime).
why_reg_cite: Governance visibility required (§741.12).

name: Regulator Notification
system_behavior: When CFP Level 2 or 3 activates, or CLF/Discount Window used/attempted, or survival < 15d (combined) or LAR < 6%, prepare and send NCUA memo.
edge_cases: After-hours trigger → notify next calendar day by 10:00 and document.
alerts_metrics: (metric.notifications\_on\_time).
why_reg_cite: Material liquidity issues must be escalated to NCUA (§741.12 purpose).

name: Federal Contingent Access
description: CLF and Fed Discount Window readiness.
system_behavior: Maintain membership/agent access to CLF and operational readiness for Discount Window; keep collateral schedules current; conduct annual test (funded or no-funds).
edge_cases: Counterparty portal outage → phone protocol.
alerts_metrics: (metric.facility\_headroom), (metric.docs\_expiring).
why_reg_cite: ≥ $250MM must document access to a federal contingent source (§741.12); CLF statute 12 USC 1795, Fed advances 12 USC 347b.

name: Collateral & Encumbrance
system_behavior: Track eligible/unencumbered balances and haircuts by counterparty; optimize pledges.

slas.description: ** Update **daily**; re-check T+0 after large moves.

edge_cases: Margin call → intraday pledge flow.
alerts_metrics: (alert.headroom\_low).
why_reg_cite: Liquidity depends on pledgeable capacity (§741.12).

name: Wholesale Deposits Guardrails
system_behavior: Allow approved listing services with tenor laddering; enforce pricing authority; stress runoff assumptions.
edge_cases: Rate spike → pause new tenors > policy.
alerts_metrics: (alert.listing\_too\_concentrated).
why_reg_cite: Manage optionality and rollover risk in non-core funding (§741.12).

name: CFP Purpose & Activation
system_behavior: Define activation Level 1 (Watch), Level 2 (Low), Level 3 (Critical) tied to [LP-05](liquidity.md#lp-05-liquid-assets-ratio-bands--watchlowcritical).

slas.description: ** Transition actions started within **2 hours** of Level 2/3.

edge_cases: False positive → revert with note.
alerts_metrics: (metric.time\_to\_activate).
why_reg_cite: Written CFP required; executable under stress (§741.12).

name: Early-Warning Indicators
system_behavior: Monitor: rapid asset growth via volatile liabilities; undue concentrations; negative press; earnings/asset-quality deterioration; rising funding costs; margin calls; early CD redemptions; correspondent line cuts.
edge_cases: Rumor-based press → escalate to Comms lead.
alerts_metrics: (alert.ewi\_red).
why_reg_cite: Detect stress early to preserve optionality (§741.12).

name: Escalation & Roles
system_behavior: CEO leads external comms; CFO runs liquidity ops; ALCO advises; Board approves extraordinary measures.
edge_cases: CEO unavailable → designate Acting CEO.
alerts_metrics: (metric.escalation\_time).
why_reg_cite: Clear decision rights enable timely action (§741.12).

name: Funding Playbooks
why_reg_cite: CFP must specify actionable funding sources (§741.12).

name: Comms & Stakeholders
system_behavior: CEO is sole spokesperson; use scripted updates to major depositors/partners; PR prepared.

slas.description: ** Issue within same day for Level 2/3.

edge_cases: Media inquiry → approved statement only.
alerts_metrics: (metric.comms\_latency).
why_reg_cite: Controlled communication mitigates run risk (§741.12 intent).

name: Regulator Liaison
system_behavior: Maintain examiner/region contacts; event memos; follow-ups; incident log.
edge_cases: Conflicting requests → elevate to CEO/Board Chair.
alerts_metrics: (metric.ncua\_sla\_met).
why_reg_cite: Maintain timely, accurate updates to NCUA (§741.12).

name: Drills & AARs
system_behavior: Annual no-funds or funded test of CLF or Discount Window; tabletop exercises; remediation tracking.
edge_cases: Missed objective → immediate corrective plan.
alerts_metrics: (metric.remediation\_ontrack).
why_reg_cite: Demonstrate operability of CFP and federal access (§741.12).

name: Documentation & Retention
system_behavior: Retain policies, limits, packs, notifications, facility tests, drills, and AARs for 10 years.
edge_cases: PII/privileged content → restricted vault.
alerts_metrics: (metric.retention\_gaps).
why_reg_cite: Evidence of compliance and effectiveness (§741.12).

name: Purpose and Scope
description: Define CFP boundaries and integration with liquidity dashboard.
system_behavior: CFP spans funding playbooks, roles, monitoring, testing, notification, and comms across \{{SCOPE\}}; integrates with dashboard in [CFP-08](contingency-funding-plan.md#cfp-08-liquidity-dashboard-and-ratios-interface).
edge_cases: New BaaS partner with novel flows → provisional overlay.
alerts_metrics: (metric.cfp\_past\_due).
why_reg_cite: Written, executable CFP required for insured credit unions (12 CFR §741.12).

name: Governance and Roles
system_behavior: CEO chairs crisis team; CFO runs liquidity ops; ALCO advises; Board authorizes extraordinary actions.
edge_cases: CEO unavailable → Acting CEO per succession matrix.
alerts_metrics: (metric.escalation\_time).
why_reg_cite: Clear decision rights and escalation enable timely action (§741.12).

name: Early Warning Indicators and Monitoring
system_behavior: Monitor: rapid asset growth from volatile liabilities; undue concentrations; negative press; deterioration in earnings/asset quality; rising funding costs; margin calls; early CD redemptions; line cuts.
edge_cases: Rumor spikes → comms prepped via [CFP-12](contingency-funding-plan.md#cfp-12-communications-and-stakeholders).
alerts_metrics: (alert.ewi\_red), (metric.ewi\_ack\_time).
why_reg_cite: Early detection preserves optionality (§741.12).

name: Activation Levels and Triggers
system_behavior:
Map metrics to Level 1 Watch, Level 2 Low, Level 3 Critical. Examples:
  * Level 1: LAR below policy floor (e.g., 10%); EWIs sustained red.
  * Level 2: LAR < 8% or survival < 30d idio / < 20d combined.
  * Level 3: LAR < 6% or outflow ≥ 40%/10d, imminent payment failure.

slas.description: ** Initiate actions within **2 hours** of Level 2/3.

edge_cases: False positives → revert with rationale.
alerts_metrics: (metric.time\_to\_activate).
why_reg_cite: CFP must specify clear activation criteria (§741.12).

name: Funding Sources Catalogue and Draw Order
system_behavior:
Maintain ordered sources with capacity, tenor, cost, and draw steps.

Internal sources:

* Cash/Fed & corporate CU balances
* Unencumbered AFS (sell/pledge)
* Saleable/participation-ready loans

External sources:

* FHLB (if eligible)
* Discount Window
* CLF
* Listing-service time deposits within policy

slas.description: ** Execute first-line actions within **2 hours** at Level 2.

edge_cases: Counterparty outage → alternate path from contact sheet.
alerts_metrics: (metric.cost\_of\_funds), (metric.headroom\_days).
why_reg_cite: CFP must enumerate actionable sources (§741.12); CLF (12 USC 1795) and Fed advances (12 USC 347b) as federal backstops.

name: Backup Funding and Collateral Readiness
system_behavior: Keep backup sources pre-documented: CLF/agent membership; DW borrower-in-custody or pledged collateral; standby listing-service capacity; correspondent fed funds lines (if applicable).
edge_cases: Security in dispute → remove from headroom until cleared.
alerts_metrics: (alert.headroom\_low), (metric.encumbrance\_pct).
why_reg_cite: Maintain contingent access to federal liquidity (§741.12; 12 USC 1795; 12 USC 347b).

name: Action Plans and Playbooks
system_behavior:
For each Level, attach a playbook with owners, steps, and timers:

Level 1 Watch

* Verify data; inventory facilities; test contacts; pre-stage collateral; tighten pricing.

Level 2 Low

* Execute ordered draws; lengthen funding tenor; pause discretionary lending; initiate depositor comms.

Level 3 Critical

* Emergency funding + asset liquidation; hourly liquidity pack; Board continuous updates.

slas.description: ** First tasks within **2 hours** at Level 2; **immediate** at Level 3.

edge_cases: Inability to draw planned facility → branch to backup in [CFP-06](contingency-funding-plan.md#cfp-06-backup-funding-and-collateral-readiness).
alerts_metrics: (metric.tasks\_on\_time), (metric.playbook\_drift).
why_reg_cite: CFP must be executable, not aspirational (§741.12).

name: Liquidity Dashboard and Ratios Interface
system_behavior: Spreadsheet/dashboard computes: LAR, cumulative mismatch gaps (O/N, 2–7d, 8–30d, 31–90d, 91–365d, >1y), survival horizon, top depositor concentrations, facility headroom, and scenario outflows.

slas.description: ** Refresh **daily by 17:00**; intraday refresh on event.

edge_cases: Missing custodian file → provisional status with flag.
alerts_metrics: (alert.band\_change), (metric.dashboard\_ontime).
why_reg_cite: Ratios/monitoring must be programmatic and timely (§741.12 intent).

name: Stress Scenarios and Survival Horizon
description: Design/refresh of stress tests and survival days.
system_behavior: Maintain base, idiosyncratic, systemic, and combined scenarios; model intraday peaks; compute survival days and required actions.
edge_cases: Facility eligibility reduced → recompute survival immediately.
alerts_metrics: (alert.survival\_below\_target).
why_reg_cite: Credible stress testing underpins CFP (§741.12).

name: Testing and Drills
system_behavior: Conduct annual no-funds (or funded) test of CLF or Discount Window; annual tabletop crisis drill; remediate gaps.
edge_cases: Partial failure → immediate corrective plan with deadline.
alerts_metrics: (metric.remediation\_ontrack).
why_reg_cite: Demonstrate operability of CFP and federal access (§741.12; 12 USC 1795; 12 USC 347b).

name: NCUA Notification Protocol
system_behavior: Notify examiner-in-charge and regional office within 24 hours when: Level 2/3 activation, use/attempted use of CLF/DW, survival < 15d (combined), or LAR < 6%.
edge_cases: After-hours trigger → by 10:00 next calendar day; document exception.
alerts_metrics: (metric.notifications\_on\_time).
why_reg_cite: Material liquidity stress must be escalated (§741.12 purpose).

name: Communications and Stakeholders
system_behavior: CEO is sole spokesperson; scripted updates to major depositors/partners; PR-ready statements.
edge_cases: Media inbound → use approved statement only.
alerts_metrics: (metric.comms\_latency).
why_reg_cite: Controlled communication mitigates run risk (§741.12 intent).

name: Documentation and Retention (10 years)
system_behavior: Retain policy versions, dashboard outputs, stress packs, notifications, facility tests, drills, AARs for 10 years.
edge_cases: PII/privileged content → restricted vault.
alerts_metrics: (metric.retention\_gaps).
why_reg_cite: Evidence of program effectiveness (§741.12).

name: Policy Objectives, Scope, and Covered Instruments
description: Anchor safety, liquidity, yield, and compliance objectives; define what assets are governed by this Policy.

triggers.human_description: Board approves new or revised Investment Policy
Compliance updates policy metadata in system

system_behavior:
* Store a single canonical Investment Policy record with effective and next-review dates.
  * Tag all portfolio positions that fall under this Policy (investments, repos, deposits, and other non-loan asset placements).

slas.description:
**
  * Max 12 months between board approvals; system warns 60 and 30 days before next\_review.

edge_cases: * If policy lapses (no approval by next\_review), investments remain governed by prior policy but system flags as non-compliant until board action. (Assumption—needs confirmation: whether CU will impose an internal trading freeze if policy expires.)
alerts_metrics:
* Metric: “Days until Investment Policy review.”
  * Alert when `days_until_review <= 60` and escalated at `<= 30`.
why_reg_cite: Part 703 requires a written investment policy consistent with the Act and other laws, addressing safety and soundness, including credit, liquidity, interest rate, and concentration risks.([GovRegs](https://www.govregs.com/regulations/expand/title12_chapterVII_part703_subpartA_section703.3?utm_source=chatgpt.com))

name: Governance, Board Oversight, and Delegations
description: Define board responsibilities, delegations, and review cadence.

triggers.human_description: CEO or Board delegates investment authority

system_behavior:
* Maintain authority matrix for investment decision-making and limits by role.
  * Link each trade and exception to an authorized approver.
edge_cases: * Emergent trade needed when approver unavailable → escalation to next senior officer in matrix; auto-email to Board Chair within 1 business day for ratification. (Assumption—needs confirmation: appetite for post-facto ratification in emergencies.)
alerts_metrics:
* Count of trades executed by each role vs. assigned limits.
  * Alerts when trade or exposure exceeds configured role limit.
why_reg_cite: Board must establish, approve, and at least annually review the investment policy, and ensure policies are within applicable law and regulations.([GovInfo](https://www.govinfo.gov/content/pkg/CFR-2018-title12-vol7/pdf/CFR-2018-title12-vol7-part703.pdf?utm_source=chatgpt.com))

name: Permissible Investments and Prohibited Activities
description: Enumerate allowed instruments and explicitly bar prohibited activities.
system_behavior:
* Enforce an allow-list of instrument types and structures mapped to Part 703 categories (e.g., U.S. Treasuries, agency securities, municipal securities, corporate bonds, deposits, CUSO investments, repos, etc.).
  * Block trade booking for instruments or activities tagged as prohibited or not permitted for the CU.
edge_cases: * Grandfathered or pilot investments handled via explicit tag `(instrument.status = 'grandfathered' / 'pilot')` with board approval reference and sunset date.
alerts_metrics:
* Number of blocked trades for policy reasons.
  * Exposure by Part 703 category for Board reporting.
why_reg_cite: Part 703 requires policies to define permissible investments and address prohibited activities/investments.([Legal Information Institute](https://www.law.cornell.edu/cfr/text/12/part-703/subpart-A?utm_source=chatgpt.com))

name: Interest Rate Risk (IRR) & ALM Integration
description: Integrate investments with IRR and ALM framework.

regulations.id: Part 703 preamble & guidance requiring IRR policy for investments

triggers.human_description: New security booked requiring IRR parameters

system_behavior:
* Capture effective duration, convexity, and cash-flow vectors for each security.
  * Feed ALM models with current and stressed cash flows for IRR measurement.
edge_cases: * Complex structured securities where vendor models differ → store both vendor and internal scenarios; flag discrepancies above defined threshold.
alerts_metrics:
* Contribution of investments to EVE and NII sensitivity vs. risk limits.
  * Breach alerts tied into [IP-12](investment.md#ip-12-ongoing-monitoring-reporting-and-stress-testing).
why_reg_cite: Part 703 and related guidance require investment policies to address interest rate risk; ALM policies may cover IRR across the balance sheet.([Federal Register](https://www.federalregister.gov/documents/2003/06/03/03-13660/investment-and-deposit-activities-and-regulatory-flexibility-program?utm_source=chatgpt.com))

name: Credit Risk Standards and Downgrade Management
description: Define minimum credit standards, analysis, monitoring, and downgrade responses.
system_behavior:
* Require a completed credit file and approval before booking eligible non-governmental investments.
  * Track annual re-analysis due dates and rating/deterioration triggers with automated tasks.
edge_cases: * Sudden credit event (e.g., default/credit watch) without updated financials → immediate placement on internal watch list; interim qualitative memo before full refresh.
alerts_metrics:
* Count and total exposure of issuers on watch list.
  * SLA adherence for annual reviews.
why_reg_cite: §703.6 requires documented credit analysis before purchase and at least annually thereafter for non-government/fully insured investments.([Legal Information Institute](https://www.law.cornell.edu/cfr/text/12/703.6?utm_source=chatgpt.com))

name: Liquidity and Marketability Limits
description: Ensure investments support daily and stressed liquidity needs.
system_behavior:
* Tag each investment with liquidity classification (e.g., HQLA-like, marketable, thinly traded, illiquid).
  * Calculate on-demand and 30-day stress liquidity capacity from investments.

slas.description:
**
  * At least monthly liquidity coverage report; more frequent in stress.

edge_cases: * Investments with no observable market price → conservative liquidity classification and haircut; escalation to ALCO. (Assumption—needs confirmation: whether CU will set explicit internal minimum share of “readily marketable” securities.)
alerts_metrics: * Breach of minimum liquid-asset buffer triggers [IP-17](investment.md#ip-17-contingency-planning-and-liquidity-stress-events).
why_reg_cite: Investments must support liquidity and CFP requirements under §741.12; policy must address liquidity risk.([Legal Information Institute](https://www.law.cornell.edu/cfr/text/12/741.12?utm_source=chatgpt.com))

name: Concentration and Counterparty Limits
description: Limit exposures by issuer, sector, instrument, and counterparty.

regulations.id: Part 703 policy requirement to address concentration risk

system_behavior:
* Maintain parameterized limits by issuer, sector, rating, product type, and counterparty.
  * Enforce soft (warning) and hard (blocking) limits at trade entry.
edge_cases: * Temporary breach due to balance-sheet shrinkage rather than new purchases → flagged but permitted with documented remediation plan.
alerts_metrics:
* Number of limit exceptions granted; trend over time.
  * Top 10 exposures vs. capital.
why_reg_cite: Investment policy must explain how concentration risk will be managed; Part 703 and NCUA guidance stress issuer, sector, and product concentration limits.([Federal Register](https://www.federalregister.gov/documents/2003/06/03/03-13660/investment-and-deposit-activities-and-regulatory-flexibility-program?utm_source=chatgpt.com))

name: Approved Brokers, Dealers, and Safekeepers
description: Approve and monitor intermediaries and safekeeping arrangements.
system_behavior:
* Maintain an approved list of broker-dealers and safekeepers with due-diligence fields and review dates.
  * Block trades with unapproved intermediaries; require safekeeper linkage for each position.
edge_cases: * Emergency trade required with non-approved dealer → flagged exception requiring CIO and Compliance approval plus prompt Board notification.
alerts_metrics:
* Intermediaries with overdue reviews.
  * Volume and P\&L by broker-dealer.
why_reg_cite: Part 703 requires due diligence and annual review of broker-dealers and safekeepers; safekeepers must be regulated and monitored under written custodial agreements.([Legal Information Institute](https://www.law.cornell.edu/cfr/text/12/703.8?utm_source=chatgpt.com))

name: Repurchase and Reverse Repurchase Agreements
description: Govern collateral, haircuts, maturity matching, and counterparties for repos.
system_behavior:
* Represent each repo/reverse repo transaction with explicit fields for collateral, haircut, counterparties, and maturity mismatch.
  * Prevent booking repos that violate internal or regulatory mismatch and collateral rules.

slas.description:
**
  * Daily mark-to-market of collateral in stress or volatile markets; at least weekly otherwise (Assumption—needs confirmation: specific revaluation cadence).

edge_cases: * Collateral value shortfall vs. haircut → auto-alert and required margin call within defined timeframe (e.g., 1 business day).
alerts_metrics:
* Total repo exposure vs. capital.
  * Count of margin calls and any collateral shortfalls.
why_reg_cite: NCUA policies on repos require safe-and-sound collateral practices and maturity match limits; borrowing repurchase maturity mismatch generally limited (e.g., 30 days) without expanded authority.([NCUA](https://ncua.gov/files/publications/irps/IRPS1985-02.pdf?utm_source=chatgpt.com))

name: Valuation, Accounting, and Fair Value
description: Set rules for GAAP compliance, fair value measurement, and OTTI assessment.
system_behavior:
* Capture book value, amortized cost, and fair value for each security.
  * Store pricing source and date; support OTTI analysis.
edge_cases: * No reliable quote available → use substantially similar security, document methodology, and apply conservative price.([GovInfo](https://www.govinfo.gov/link/cfr/12/703?link-type=pdf\&sectionnum=12\&year=mostrecent\&utm_source=chatgpt.com))
alerts_metrics:
* Percentage of positions using non-primary pricing sources.
  * Unrealized loss as % of capital by product type.
why_reg_cite: §703.4 requires GAAP-compliant recordkeeping; §703.11 requires at least monthly fair value determination, with independent assessment of pricing sources.([Legal Information Institute](https://www.law.cornell.edu/cfr/text/12/703.4?utm_source=chatgpt.com))

name: Pre-Purchase Due Diligence and Exceptions
description: Define documentation, approvals, and exception handling before purchase.
system_behavior:
* Enforce a pre-trade checklist and approval workflow before any new investment is booked (other than U.S. government/fully insured instruments).
  * Require linkage of each trade ticket to a credit memo and valuation support.

slas.description:
**
  * Pre-purchase checklist completed and approved before trade date.
  * Exceptions (where permitted) documented before settlement.

edge_cases: * Same issuer/add-on purchases within pre-defined limits may allow streamlined checklist referencing prior memo, but still require documented tie-back. (Assumption—needs confirmation: specific thresholds for streamlined process.)
alerts_metrics:
* Ratio of trades with exceptions to total trades.
  * Time to complete pre-purchase reviews.
why_reg_cite: §703.6 mandates documented credit analysis before purchase (with limited exceptions), and NCUA guidance requires independent risk assessment beyond ratings.([Legal Information Institute](https://www.law.cornell.edu/cfr/text/12/703.6?utm_source=chatgpt.com))

name: Ongoing Monitoring, Reporting, and Stress Testing
description: Require ongoing performance, risk, and compliance reporting to ALCO and Board.
system_behavior:
* Generate recurring reports on portfolio composition, duration, liquidity, unrealized gains/losses, limit adherence, and risk metrics.
  * Implement stress scenarios (rate shocks, spread widening, liquidity stress).
edge_cases: * Data gaps or vendor outages → contingency reporting with clearly marked estimates and follow-up corrections.
alerts_metrics:
* On-time completion of required reports.
  * Number of open remediation items from stress tests.
why_reg_cite: §703.12 and related sections require at least monthly written reports on investments and monitoring of risk; liquidity rule requires CFP and stress planning.([Legal Information Institute](https://www.law.cornell.edu/cfr/text/12/703.11?utm_source=chatgpt.com))

name: Performance Measurement and Benchmarks
description: Align portfolio performance assessment with risk and policy limits.
system_behavior:
* Attribute portfolio total return and income relative to chosen benchmarks by segment (e.g., Treasuries vs. agencies vs. municipals).
  * Ensure performance targets do not create incentives to breach risk limits.
edge_cases: * Benchmark index changes → require ALCO approval and documented rationale.
alerts_metrics: * Tracking error and risk-adjusted return vs. policy; flags if performance is achieved via higher-than-approved risk.
why_reg_cite: While not prescribing specific benchmarks, NCUA expects investment activities to be consistent with safe and sound practices and risk tolerance; performance must be evaluated in that context.([Federal Register](https://www.federalregister.gov/documents/2003/06/03/03-13660/investment-and-deposit-activities-and-regulatory-flexibility-program?utm_source=chatgpt.com))

name: Trade Execution, Controls, and Segregation of Duties
description: Ensure safe trade processing, confirmations, and reconciliations.

regulations.id: [12 CFR §703.4(b)](https://www.law.cornell.edu/cfr/text/12/703.4) (internal controls and segregation of duties).([Legal Information Institute](https://www.law.cornell.edu/cfr/text/12/703.4?utm_source=chatgpt.com))

system_behavior:
* Segregate responsibilities among trade initiation, approval, confirmation, settlement, and accounting.
  * Enforce dual-control for trade entry and post-trade reconciliation.

slas.description:
**
  * Trade confirmation and reconciliation within T+1 (Assumption—needs confirmation: targeted T+1 vs. T+2 SLA).

edge_cases: * Same person attempts to both enter and approve trade → system blocks and logs violation attempt.
alerts_metrics:
* Number of reconciliation breaks and age distribution.
  * Attempts at violating segregation-of-duties rules.
why_reg_cite: §703.4 requires policies to address internal controls and segregation of duties in investment activities.([Legal Information Institute](https://www.law.cornell.edu/cfr/text/12/703.4?utm_source=chatgpt.com))

name: Recordkeeping and Documentation Retention
description: Define minimum records, retention periods, and accessibility.
system_behavior:
* Store and index all required documents: trade tickets, confirmations, credit memos, research, approvals, safekeeping statements, and reports.
  * Support retention schedules consistent with regulatory and internal requirements.
edge_cases: * Missing or damaged records → flag, attempt reconstruction, and document limitation for audit/examiners.
alerts_metrics:
* Missing required documents by trade/position.
  * Imminent retention expiry requiring legal hold assessment.
why_reg_cite: §703.4 sets recordkeeping and documentation requirements for investments and related activities.([Legal Information Institute](https://www.law.cornell.edu/cfr/text/12/703.4?utm_source=chatgpt.com))

name: Training, Competency, and Conflicts of Interest
description: Set minimum training standards and manage conflicts.
system_behavior:
* Track training completion for staff and board involved in investment oversight.
  * Maintain conflict-of-interest disclosures for key personnel and vendors.
edge_cases: * Non-completion of training or COI certification → auto-suspension of investment system access until resolved.
alerts_metrics:
* Training completion rates.
  * COI issues logged and resolved.
why_reg_cite: Part 703 requires management competence; conflicts of interest in investment activities are explicitly prohibited under §703.17.([Legal Information Institute](https://www.law.cornell.edu/cfr/text/12/part-703/subpart-A?utm_source=chatgpt.com))

name: Contingency Planning and Liquidity Stress Events
description: Specify stress tests and playbooks for adverse markets.
system_behavior:
* Maintain predefined liquidity stress scenarios (e.g., deposit run, market stress, combined).
  * Map investment liquidation hierarchy and playbooks tied into the CFP.
edge_cases: * Market dislocation where planned sales not feasible at modeled haircuts → require revised plan and Board communication.
alerts_metrics:
* Liquidity coverage ratios under base and stress vs. policy minimums.
  * Time to execute simulated CFP actions.
why_reg_cite: §741.12 requires a written CFP and planning for liquidity stress; investment portfolio is a core contingent liquidity source.([Legal Information Institute](https://www.law.cornell.edu/cfr/text/12/741.12?utm_source=chatgpt.com))

name: Policy Review, Amendments, and Version Control
description: Maintain compliant annual review and change control.
system_behavior:
* Maintain full version history, redlines, and approval metadata for the Investment Policy.
  * Drive review tasks and track completion.
edge_cases: * Emergency interim updates (e.g., new prohibited instrument) requiring prompt Board ratification at next meeting.
alerts_metrics:
* “Days since last Board approval.”
  * Count of outstanding follow-up items from prior reviews.
why_reg_cite: §703.3 requires the Board to review the investment policy at least annually; §741.3 ties investment policies to insurability criteria.([GovInfo](https://www.govinfo.gov/content/pkg/CFR-2018-title12-vol7/pdf/CFR-2018-title12-vol7-part703.pdf?utm_source=chatgpt.com))

name: Governance & Roles
description: Define Board→Management accountability and IMT roles.
system_behavior: Maintain a living BCP/DR plan with clear owners; auto-collect training/tests; generate Board reports at least annually.
edge_cases: CEO unavailable → pre-delegated acting IC (Incident Commander).
alerts_metrics: Past-due reviews; IMT coverage %; training completion %.
why_reg_cite: Board must approve/oversee continuity plans; management implements and reports. [12 CFR §701.4](https://www.ecfr.gov/current/title-12/part-701/section-701.4).

name: Risk Assessment (Hazards by Region)
description: Identify/score scenarios (e.g., CA wildfire, TX tornado).
system_behavior: Maintain threat register (wildfire/smoke—CA; earthquake—CA; tornado—TX; flood; grid outage; cyber; pandemic).
edge_cases: Multi-region concurrent events → separate ICs with unified command.
alerts_metrics: % hazards with assigned mitigations; open CAP items.
why_reg_cite: Records and operations must be protectable against catastrophic events; identify risks by geography. [12 CFR Part 749](https://www.ecfr.gov/current/title-12/part-749).

name: Business Impact Analysis (BIA)
system_behavior: Catalog services and rank by member impact/regulatory dependency.
edge_cases: Conflicts → COO arbitrates using decision priorities.
alerts_metrics:
% services with current BIA; % with tested runbooks.

> Illustrative BIA Ranking (Assumption—needs confirmation):
>
> * Member Authentication/Account Access
> * Card Processing
> * Core Banking/ACH/Wires
> * Payments & Deposits
> * Lending Origination/Servicing
> * Contact Center
> * Reporting/Reg Filings
why_reg_cite: Identify vital records and functions to set RTO/RPO and recovery order. [12 CFR Part 749](https://www.ecfr.gov/current/title-12/part-749).

name: Training, Testing & Exercises
description: Train staff; validate plans; Board reporting.
system_behavior: Annual exercise plan (orientation, tabletop, functional); track completion; feed gaps to CAP.
edge_cases: Real incident substitutes for test if PIR completed.
alerts_metrics: Training completion %; time-to-CAP close.
why_reg_cite: Programs must be trained, tested, and validated; include incident response practices. [12 CFR Part 748 Appendix A](https://www.ecfr.gov/current/title-12/part-748/appendix-Appendix%20A%20to%20Part%20748).

name: Monitoring, Detection & Severity
description: Detect incidents; classify severity; assign commander.
system_behavior: Central on-call with severity matrix (SEV-1 to SEV-4); weather/cyber/vendor monitors; paging.

slas.description: ** IC assigned ≤5m for SEV-1; initial comms ≤15m.

edge_cases: Conflicting alerts → highest impact drives SEV.
alerts_metrics: MTTA/MTTR; false positive rate.
why_reg_cite: Safeguards require monitoring and prompt incident identification/response. [12 CFR Part 748 Appendix A](https://www.ecfr.gov/current/title-12/part-748/appendix-Appendix%20A%20to%20Part%20748).

name: Incident Declaration & Initial Actions
system_behavior: “First hour” checklist: safety, stabilize, scope, assign roles, notify, set cadence.
edge_cases: Multi-tenant vendor incident → join vendor bridge; retain independence on comms.
alerts_metrics: % incidents with complete first-hour checklist.
why_reg_cite: Structured incident response is required to protect member information and services. [12 CFR Part 748 Appendix A](https://www.ecfr.gov/current/title-12/part-748/appendix-Appendix%20A%20to%20Part%20748).

name: Data Backup & Restore; RTO/RPO
system_behavior: Tiered backups (critical, high, standard); immutable/offsite copies; periodic restore tests.
edge_cases: Crypto-lock events → use clean point-in-time per RPO.
alerts_metrics: Backup success rate; time-to-restore vs RTO.
why_reg_cite: Preserve/recover vital records; meet RTO/RPO by service tier. [12 CFR Part 749](https://www.ecfr.gov/current/title-12/part-749).

name: Alternate Site & Remote Operations
system_behavior: Pre-approved remote posture (VPN, MFA, split DNS), hot/virtual site options, minimum staffing lists.

slas.description: ** Alt site/remote ready ≤8h; full critical ops ≤24h.

edge_cases: Power/internet regional outage → cellular failover kits.
alerts_metrics: % essential staff remote-ready; VPN capacity headroom.
why_reg_cite: Ensure operations can continue when facilities are unavailable. [12 CFR Part 749](https://www.ecfr.gov/current/title-12/part-749).

name: Major IT Failure Response
system_behavior: Runbook for core/cloud outages: detect, isolate blast radius, rollback/failover, communicate.

slas.description: ** IC ≤5m; member status ≤15m; failover per BIA tier.

edge_cases: Partial data divergence → choose authoritative ledger and reconcile.
alerts_metrics: MTTR; % successful failovers; incident comms latency.
why_reg_cite: Safeguards mandate timely containment and continuity; coordinate with service providers. [12 CFR Part 748 Appendix A](https://www.ecfr.gov/current/title-12/part-748/appendix-Appendix%20A%20to%20Part%20748).

name: Incident Response (Privacy/Security)
system_behavior: Contain, eradicate, recover; legal/regulatory consult; notification decisioning; forensics; evidence chain.

slas.description: ** Containment start ≤1h; counsel consult ≤24h; notices per law.

edge_cases: Third-party breach without final RCA → send interim member comms.
alerts_metrics: Time to containment; completeness of affected records.
why_reg_cite: Implement GLBA-aligned incident response, including service provider coordination and member notification where appropriate. [12 CFR Part 748 Appendix A](https://www.ecfr.gov/current/title-12/part-748/appendix-Appendix%20A%20to%20Part%20748).

name: Communications & Notification Tree
system_behavior: Maintain contact trees for employees, Board, regulators, vendors, media; predefined status page playbooks.

slas.description: ** First internal alert ≤15m; regulator per law/policy.

edge_cases: Comms platform failure → backup channels (SMS/phone tree).
alerts_metrics: Delivery success; time-to-first-message.
why_reg_cite: Board accountability for orderly operations and delegation includes communication plans. [12 CFR §701.4](https://www.ecfr.gov/current/title-12/part-701/section-701.4).

name: People Continuity & Pandemic
description: Staffing plans; split teams; HR triggers.
system_behavior: Identify essential roles; cross-train; implement split teams/remote work; track absenteeism.
edge_cases: Caregiver concentration risk → augment with contractors.
alerts_metrics: Essential coverage %; cross-train coverage %.
why_reg_cite: Preparedness for people outages supports continuity. [12 CFR Part 749](https://www.ecfr.gov/current/title-12/part-749).

name: Post-Incident Review (PIR)
system_behavior: Conduct PIR with root cause, what worked/failed, corrective action plan (CAP), retest.
edge_cases: Vendor RCA delayed → issue interim PIR; update upon receipt.
alerts_metrics: % PIR on time; CAP closure velocity.
why_reg_cite: Board oversight expects effectiveness assessment and improvement loop. [12 CFR §701.4](https://www.ecfr.gov/current/title-12/part-701/section-701.4).

name: Vendor Contingency Management
system_behavior: Maintain vendor BCP/IR attestations, SLAs, and failover plans; define exit/failover criteria.
edge_cases: Shared failure modes (e.g., same cloud region) → diversify.
alerts_metrics: % critical vendors with current DR evidence; SLA breaches to PIRs.
why_reg_cite: Safeguards require oversight of service providers’ incident response and continuity capabilities. [12 CFR Part 748 Appendix A](https://www.ecfr.gov/current/title-12/part-748/appendix-Appendix%20A%20to%20Part%20748).

name: Enterprise Risk Appetite Statement
description: Define how much risk the Credit Union is willing to accept by risk type and align ERM to strategy.
system_behavior:
* Store a single authoritative enterprise risk appetite statement document and structured data (per risk category) in the risk system.
  * Enforce versioning, effective/expiry dates, and approval history (Board, committees).
  * Lock appetite settings from edit except by authorized roles (CRO + delegated risk officers) with electronic approvals.
edge_cases:
* Overlapping or conflicting appetite statements (e.g., enterprise vs. specific program) must be flagged for CRO resolution.
  * If a new risk category is created without appetite defined, system must label it “UNDEFINED APPETITE” and prevent marking residual risk as “within appetite”.
alerts_metrics:
* Count of risk categories with defined vs. undefined appetite.
  * % of appetite statements reviewed on time.
  * Automatic alerts to CRO and Corporate Secretary when appetite review is overdue.
why_reg_cite: Align risk-taking with the Credit Union’s mission and strategic plan and set clear boundaries for risk, consistent with NCUA ERM expectations and safety-and-soundness standards (including [12 CFR Part 741](https://www.law.cornell.edu/cfr/text/12/part-741) and related guidance).

name: Risk Taxonomy & Categories
description: Define consistent risk categories covering financial, operational, compliance, technology, model, strategic, and reputational risk.
system_behavior:
* Maintain a canonical list of risk categories and subcategories (financial, operational, compliance, technology, model, strategic, reputational, etc.).
  * Enforce selection from this taxonomy for every risk entry in the risk register, KRI, and incident record.
edge_cases:
* Legacy records mapped to deprecated categories must retain historical mapping while being reclassified into current taxonomy.
  * System must prevent creation of a risk record with a free-text category not in taxonomy.
alerts_metrics:
* % of risk records mapped to deprecated categories.
  * Count of categories/subcategories per risk type (to avoid unnecessary proliferation).
why_reg_cite: A clear, enterprise-wide risk taxonomy is a core component of ERM and risk culture; it enables consistent assessment across risk types as expected by NCUA ERM guidance.

name: Risk Scoring Matrix & Rating Scale
description: Define the 5x5 likelihood/impact matrix and scoring rules used across the Credit Union.
system_behavior:
* Implement a 5x5 likelihood vs. impact matrix, with numerical scores and qualitative labels for both axes and overall risk rating (e.g., Very Low, Low, Moderate, High, Very High).
  * Enforce use of this matrix for all enterprise risk assessments (financial, operational, etc.) unless explicitly exempted by CRO.

slas.description: * Risk scoring scale reviewed at least every three years or when major changes occur in business model or regulatory expectations.

edge_cases:
* If likelihood/impact inputs are missing, system blocks saving the assessment.
  * If risk is purely qualitative (e.g., emerging risk), system requires default scoring rules approved by ERM.
alerts_metrics:
* Distribution of residual risk ratings across categories (heatmap).
  * Count and percentage of risks rated High/Very High inherent vs. residual.
why_reg_cite: A consistent scoring methodology is required for meaningful, comparable risk assessment across risk types; aligns with NCUA expectations for effective risk management and internal control frameworks.

name: Risk Assessment & Register Maintenance
description: Ensure all material risks are assessed, rated, owned, and kept current in a centralized register.
system_behavior:
* Maintain a centralized enterprise risk register that holds all material risks, their ratings, controls, and owners.
  * Support risk lifecycle states (identified, assessed, mitigated, monitored, closed).
  * Integrate with incident, model risk, vendor risk, and compliance issue tracking to avoid duplicate risk records.
edge_cases:
* Duplicate risk records must be merged with clear history.
  * Risks with no owner or no review within SLA are automatically flagged.
alerts_metrics:
* ## and % of risks with overdue reassessments.
  * Top 10 residual risks by score and trend over time.
why_reg_cite: NCUA expects credit unions to identify, measure, monitor, and control risk; a central risk register and periodic assessments are foundational to this obligation.

name: Key Risk Indicators & Thresholds
description: Set measurable indicators and thresholds that operationalize risk appetite and trigger escalation.
system_behavior:
* Allow definition of KRIs linked to specific risks and categories, with numeric thresholds aligned to appetite (green/amber/red).
  * Support automated and manual data feeds; compute status vs. thresholds and aggregate for dashboards.

slas.description: * KRIs refreshed at configured frequency; system flags stale data if no update received within 1.5x expected interval.

edge_cases:
* KRIs with incomplete thresholds or missing direction must not be marked “Active”.
  * Manual overrides of KRI values must be documented and auditable.
alerts_metrics:
* Count of red and amber KRIs by category.
  * % of KRIs with stale data.
  * Time from breach detection to initial triage.
why_reg_cite: KRIs are a standard tool in ERM to monitor whether actual risk levels remain within appetite and to provide early warning of emerging issues.

name: Risk Appetite Breach Escalation & Incident Management
description: Define pathways and timelines for escalating, triaging, and resolving appetite breaches.
system_behavior:
* Detect when risk assessments, KRIs, or quantitative limits indicate a position is outside approved appetite.
  * Automatically generate a breach record with severity, initial triage, and escalation workflow.
  * Track remediation activities until risk is back within appetite or formally accepted (RA-07).

slas.description:
* Initial triage and notification to CRO within **1 business day** for Major/Critical breaches.
  * Presentation to appropriate committee (e.g., Risk Committee, ALCO) within **30 calendar days** for M

edge_cases:
* Multiple related breaches (e.g., repeated KRIs) may be linked to a single “master breach” to avoid noise while maintaining paper trail.
  * If a breach cannot be remediated within target date, RA-07 risk acceptance workflow must be triggered.
alerts_metrics:
* ## of open breaches by severity and age.
  * Breaches per quarter by category.
  * Average time-to-triage and time-to-close.
why_reg_cite: Safety-and-soundness expectations require timely identification, escalation, and remediation of risk positions outside appetite; effective escalation pathways are essential to Board oversight and supervisory confidence.

name: Risk Acceptance & Exceptions
description: Govern who may accept or temporarily tolerate risk positions above appetite and how.
system_behavior:
* Provide structured risk acceptance workflow tied to specific risks and breaches.
  * Record approver, rationale, conditions, and expiry date; track renewal and revocation.

slas.description:
* Risk acceptance requests decided within 30 calendar days of submission.
  * System issues alerts 30 and 7 days before acceptance expiry.

edge_cases:
* No “indefinite” acceptances: system must require explicit end date; renewals create a new record referencing prior acceptance.
  * If acceptance expires without renewal, risk must revert to “breach” status until within appetite.
alerts_metrics:
* Count of active acceptances by severity and category.
  * % of acceptances renewed vs. remediated.
  * Acceptances that lapsed without timely review.
why_reg_cite: Some risk positions may remain outside appetite for a period when remediation is not immediately feasible. Formal risk acceptance ensures transparency, documented rationale, and Board-aligned governance.

name: Risk Reporting & Governance Oversight
description: Ensure timely, transparent risk reporting to management and the Board, and alignment with 3-lines-of-defense.

regulations.id: SR 11-7 concepts for model-based risk reporting

system_behavior:
* Generate standard risk reports and dashboards for management committees and the Board (e.g., heatmaps, top risks, breach summaries, KRI trends).
  * Support drill-down from aggregate views to underlying risk assessments, breaches, and acceptances.
edge_cases:
* Conflicting numbers between systems (e.g., ALM vs. risk register) must be reconciled or explicitly explained in the report.
  * If data is incomplete, report must contain visible caveats rather than silently omitting segments.
alerts_metrics:
* Report delivery timeliness against committee calendars.
  * Number of material issues requiring Board attention per period.
  * Completion of Board-directed follow-ups.
why_reg_cite: Effective governance requires reliable, concise risk reporting to management and the Board, including aggregate views and trends, consistent with NCUA ERM guidance and Board responsibilities for safety and soundness.

name: Governance & Ownership
system_behavior: Maintain policy artifact; require annual board approval; produce quarterly CDD metrics aligned to FFIEC themes (alerts, SARs, ECDD coverage, model/change controls); route escalations to Management Risk Committee and Board Risk Committee.
edge_cases: Interim updates for regulatory or FFIEC manual changes (out-of-cycle board ratification).
alerts_metrics: Policy age; open issues past due; % actions closed on time.
why_reg_cite: Board-approved BSA/AML program; designated BSA Officer; internal controls, independent testing, training. 12 CFR §748.2. FFIEC Manual informs examiner expectations for CDD governance and reporting.

name: Scope & Applicability
system_behavior: Enforce CDD gates on all onboarding APIs (first-party and \{{Program\_Manager\_1\}}/\{{Program\_Manager\_2\}}) before account open; include non-face-to-face per FFIEC risk factors.
edge_cases: Pilot/limited release still requires gates.
alerts_metrics: % launches with CDD sign-off.
why_reg_cite: BSA program must cover all lines and delivery channels. 12 CFR §748.2.

name: Member Risk Profiling (Nature & Purpose)
system_behavior: Capture expected activity; assign initial risk score; store rationale; support refresh.
edge_cases: Thin-file; non-resident; PEP indicator.
alerts_metrics: % files with complete MRP; risk score distribution.
why_reg_cite: Understand nature/purpose; establish risk profiles; ongoing CDD. 31 CFR §1020.210. FFIEC Manual outlines factors for risk rating and documentation quality.

name: CIP & Identity Verification
system_behavior: Orchestrate KYC via \{{Primary\_KYC\_Vendor\_Name\}} with fallback to \{{Secondary\_Data\_Providers\}}; apply documentary/non-documentary methods; block open until pass.
edge_cases: Mismatches; fraud signals; manual review path with dual control.
alerts_metrics: False-positive/negative rates; manual review rate; aging queue.
why_reg_cite: Identify & verify customers; recordkeeping. 31 CFR §1020.220. FFIEC Manual provides examiner expectations for documentary vs non-documentary methods and risk-based variation.

name: Beneficial Ownership (Legal Entities)
system_behavior: Require BO form; validate IDs of BOs; store control person data; reconcile to KYB.

slas.description: ** Complete **before** business account opening; refresh on ownership change event.

edge_cases: Trusts; layered entities; exemptions (document rationale).
alerts_metrics: % BO verified; exception count.
why_reg_cite: Collect & verify BO (≥25% owners + one control). 31 CFR §1010.230.

name: Sanctions & Adverse Media Screening
system_behavior: Screen applicants and ongoing members via \{{Sanctions\_List\_Provider\}} and \{{Adverse\_Media\_Provider\}}; auto-hold on potential match; adjudication workflow.

slas.description: ** Potential match held immediately; adjudicate within **1 business day**.

edge_cases: Common-name matches; transliteration; fuzzy logic collisions.
alerts_metrics: Hit rate; average time to disposition; % confirmed.
why_reg_cite: Risk-based screening is an AML control. 31 CFR §1020.210. FFIEC Manual covers sanctions screening expectations and ongoing monitoring ties.

name: ECDD Triggers & Actions
system_behavior: On trigger, generate ECDD checklist; require senior approval; increase monitoring cadence.

slas.description: ** Open ECDD within **1 business day** of trigger; complete within **5 business days** or document extension.

edge_cases: False positives from vendor models—require human rationale.
alerts_metrics: % ECDD timely; keep/exit ratios.
why_reg_cite: Higher-risk relationships require ECDD. 31 CFR §1020.210. FFIEC outlines risk factors (products, services, geographies, customers) and documentation standards.

name: Rejection & Exit Thresholds
system_behavior: Enforce configurable thresholds aligned to \{{Risk\_Appetite\_Summary\}}; block onboarding or trigger exit.

slas.description: ** Decision within **2 business days** of final facts.

edge_cases: Critical customer impact—offer controlled wind-down.
alerts_metrics: Decline rate by reason; exit count; appeals.
why_reg_cite: Maintain safe and sound operations. 12 CFR §748.2. FFIEC provides examples of risk indicators supporting deny/exit decisions.

name: Ongoing Monitoring & Reviews
system_behavior: Compare observed activity to MRP; surface anomalies; schedule refresh per \{{Review\_Frequencies\}}; integrate case management and SAR workflow.

slas.description: ** Alert triage within **1 business day**; periodic refresh per risk tier (e.g., high 12m / med 24m / low 36m—confirm in \{{Review\_Frequencies\}}).

edge_cases: Seasonality; one-off spikes; partner-driven campaigns.
alerts_metrics: Alert-to-SAR conversion; refresh compliance %; case aging.
why_reg_cite: Ongoing monitoring & updates to risk profiles. 31 CFR §1020.210. FFIEC Manual details alert handling, case documentation, SAR decisions, and periodic reviews.

name: Vendor & Program-Manager Oversight
system_behavior: Maintain live register; track SLAs/KPIs; enforce audit/exam rights; require incident-notice terms compatible with §748.1(c) 72-hour rule; document model governance for vendor decisioning (inputs, thresholds, backtesting/drift).
edge_cases: Sub-processor changes; model drift at vendor—trigger re-validation; cross-border data transfers.
alerts_metrics: SLA adherence %; issue backlog; model drift indicators.
why_reg_cite: CU retains accountability for outsourced BSA functions. 12 CFR §748.2.
